title: Lines, Containers, Headings, Paragraphs, Lists, Quotes, and Fenced Code · Behavior Case: Heading Paragraph
summary: Blank lines erase structure, list markers nest by guesswork, headings skip source evidence, a fence closes with the wrong marker, and paragraphs swallow following blocks. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the heading paragraph increment in line-oriented block parser with explicit continuation rules. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Parse ATX headings and paragraphs with closing-marker, interruption, inline-content, and empty-heading evidence.
Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-block-parsing-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-block-parsing-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_block_parsing_5(lines: int = 28, consumed_lines: int = 28, blocks: int = 9, invalid_containers: int = 0, unclosed_fences: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if lines < 1 or consumed_lines < 0:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-count"
    if consumed_lines != lines:
        return False, consumed_lines, "line-consumption"
    if blocks < 1 or blocks > lines:
        return False, blocks, "block-shape"
    if invalid_containers > 0 or unclosed_fences > 0:
        return False, invalid_containers + unclosed_fences, "block-boundary"
    assert consumed_lines == lines
    return True, blocks, "bounded-block-tree"

Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the heading paragraph defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Heading Paragraph model · Theory Heading Paragraph
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Heading Paragraph: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 5949 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to parse headings, paragraphs, containers, and fenced code with explicit line consumption; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse ATX headings and paragraphs with closing-marker, interruption, inline-content, and empty-heading evidence.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Heading Paragraph model · Theory Heading Paragraph.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Heading Paragraph model · Theory Heading Paragraph, keep Heading Paragraph connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Parse ATX headings and paragraphs with closing-marker, interruption, inline-content, and empty-heading evidence.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse ATX headings and paragraphs with closing-marker, interruption, inline-content, and empty-heading evidence.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Heading Paragraph model · Theory Heading Paragraph, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A line starting with a hash is always a heading.
